notes: Batons may be legal to own in Germany, but carrying them in public is restricted and may require justification.
Kubotans are generally legal in Germany as simple everyday items
Self-defence keychains are legal if they are simple tools, but weaponised designs may be restricted under weapons law.
Carrying a sword in public in Germany is restricted and generally requires a legitimate reason such as transport or events.
Tasers are generally restricted in Germany and are not legal for civilian possession.
Pepper spray is legal in Germany as animal repellent but carrying it for self-defence against people can create legal risk
Switchblades are restricted in Germany, with legality depending on blade length and opening mechanism.
Butterfly knives are prohibited weapons in Germany and cannot be legally owned or carried.
Stun guns are legal in Germany only if they meet strict certification requirements and are approved for civilian use.
Brass knuckles are prohibited weapons in Germany and cannot be legally owned or carried.
